name: BunnyCDN API
what_it_does: Provides affordable CDN delivery, video streaming (Bunny Stream), and object storage (Bunny Storage) via a unified REST API.
use_cases: Serving static assets and media files via CDN edge nodes
Video transcoding and adaptive streaming with Bunny Stream
Cost-efficient object storage as a Backblaze B2 or S3 alternative
Pull and push zones for caching origin content at the edge
Image optimization and resizing at the CDN layer
not_for: Teams needing ISO 27001 or FedRAMP compliance
best_when: You need cheap, reliable CDN or object storage with a well-documented API and minimal vendor lock-in, especially for indie/startup projects
avoid_when: You need enterprise-grade SLAs with dedicated support, or require strict compliance certifications beyond GDPR

auth.notes: AccessKey header used for API authentication; separate storage zone passwords for Bunny Storage endpoints

pricing.paid_tiers: $0.01/GB bandwidth for US/EU, $6/TB/month for storage, Bunny Stream from $0.015/min encoded
pricing.notes: Among the most affordable CDNs on the market; no free tier but very low minimum costs

performance.rate_limits: No published hard rate limits on API; CDN delivery scales automatically

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns standard HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies; messages are generally clear
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Storage PUT operations are idempotent; CDN purge calls are safe to retry
known_agent_gotchas: Separate API keys per product area (CDN, Storage, Stream) require careful credential management
Storage API uses a different base URL than the management API
CDN cache purge can take 30-60 seconds to propagate globally
Bunny Stream video processing is asynchronous — agents must poll for completion

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced; API key auth with no scope granularity; storage passwords are zone-level
